title: oxalis caerulea
intro: Oxalis caerulea, also known as the purple wood sorrel, is a type of flowering plant that belongs to the oxalis genus and is native to the Americas. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly its ability to act as a diuretic and to help lower blood pressure. The health benefits of oxalis caerulea include its potential to help with urinary tract issues, kidney stones, and even as a natural remedy for fever and inflammation. The plant can be prepared as an herbal tea, tincture, or poultice, and is often used in folk medicine to promote overall health and well-being.
